content:
# API Authentication (v1 — DEPRECATED)

CloudSync Pro API v1 uses Basic Authentication. Include your username and password in the Authorization header:

```
Authorization: Basic base64(username:password)
```

Example:
```bash
curl -H 'Authorization: Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz' https://api.cloudsync.example/v1/files
```

Rate limit: 100 requests per minute per user.

**Note**: API v1 will be sunset on March 31, 2025. Please migrate to v2 OAuth-based auth.

# API Authentication (v2 — Current)

CloudSync Pro API v2 uses OAuth 2.0 with API keys for server-to-server and OAuth flows for user-facing apps.

## Server-to-Server (API Key)
1. Generate an API key: Admin > API > Keys > Create Key
2. Include in requests:
```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

## User-Facing Apps (OAuth 2.0)
1. Register your app: Admin > API > OAuth Apps > Register
2. Use Authorization Code flow with PKCE
3. Token endpoint: POST /oauth/token
4. Scopes: files.read, files.write, admin.read, admin.write

Rate limit: 1000 requests per minute per API key. Burst: 50 requests per second.

All API keys can be scoped to specific namespaces and operations for least-privilege access.

# Sharing Files and Folders

CloudSync Pro offers multiple ways to share content:

## Share Links
- Right-click any file/folder > Share > Create Link
- Options: View-only, Download allowed, Edit allowed
- Security: password protection, expiration date, watermarking (PDF only)
- Analytics: track views and downloads in Share > Link Analytics

## Team Sharing
- Add users/groups directly: Right-click > Share > Add People
- Permission levels: Viewer, Commenter, Editor, Admin
- Inheritance: subfolder permissions inherit from parent by default

## External Collaboration
- Requires Enterprise plan or external sharing enabled by admin
- External users get limited guest accounts (no storage quota)
- Admin can restrict external sharing to specific domains

## Best Practices
- Use Team Folders for ongoing collaboration
- Use Share Links for one-off sharing
- Set expiration on all external share links
- Review shared content monthly via Admin > Sharing > Audit

# Desktop Sync Setup (v1 — DEPRECATED)

CloudSync Pro desktop sync v1 uses a simple folder mirror approach.

## Setup
1. Install the desktop client from cloudsync.example/download
2. Sign in with your credentials
3. Choose a local sync folder (default: ~/CloudSync)
4. All cloud files will download to this folder

## Limitations
- Full sync only (no selective sync)
- No conflict resolution (last-write-wins)
- Maximum 50,000 files
- No support for symlinks or hardlinks

**This sync engine is replaced by v2 Smart Sync. Upgrade your desktop client to v4.0+.**

# Desktop Sync Setup (v2 Smart Sync — Current)

CloudSync Pro Smart Sync keeps files in the cloud and downloads on-demand.

## Setup
1. Install desktop client v4.0+ from cloudsync.example/download
2. Sign in (SSO supported)
3. Choose sync mode:
   - **Smart Sync (default)**: Files appear as placeholders; download when opened
   - **Selective Sync**: Choose specific folders to keep offline
   - **Full Sync**: Mirror everything locally (legacy mode)

## Features
- Handles 1M+ files efficiently
- 3-way conflict resolution with visual merge tool
- Bandwidth throttling: Preferences > Network > Limits
- Exclude patterns: Preferences > Sync > Exclude (e.g., node_modules, .git)
- LAN sync: auto-detects peers on local network for faster initial sync

## Troubleshooting
- Stuck on 'Preparing': Delete watcher.db from app data folder and restart
- High CPU: Add exclude patterns for large directories
- Slow sync: Check bandwidth throttling settings and network conditions

# User Management Guide

## Adding Users
- Manual: Admin > Users > Add User (email invite)
- Bulk: Admin > Users > Import CSV (columns: email, name, role, groups)
- Auto-provisioning: Enable via SSO settings (Admin > Identity > Auto-provision)

## Roles
- **Member**: Standard file access based on folder permissions
- **Power User**: Member + can create Team Folders and share externally
- **Admin**: Full admin panel access
- **Super Admin**: Admin + can manage other admins and billing

## Groups
- Create groups: Admin > Groups > Create
- Sync from AD/LDAP: Admin > Identity > Directory Sync
- Use groups for permissions instead of individual users

## Offboarding
- Suspend user: Admin > Users > [user] > Suspend (preserves files)
- Transfer files: Admin > Users > [user] > Transfer Ownership
- Delete user: Removes account, transferred files remain

## Monitoring
- Active users: Admin > Dashboard > Usage
- Login activity: Admin > Audit > Logins
- Storage per user: Admin > Users > sort by Storage Used

# Backup and Recovery

## Automatic Backups
CloudSync Pro maintains:
- **File versions**: Every save creates a version (configurable retention: 30/60/90/unlimited days)
- **Deleted file recovery**: Trash retains files for 30 days (Enterprise: 90 days)
- **Geo-redundant storage**: All data replicated across 3 regions

## Manual Backup
- Export all data: Admin > Data > Export (generates downloadable archive)
- API backup: GET /api/v2/backup/snapshot (creates point-in-time snapshot)
- Scheduled exports: Admin > Data > Scheduled Export (daily/weekly to S3, GCS, or Azure Blob)

## Recovery Procedures
- Single file: Right-click > Version History > Restore
- Deleted file: Trash > Right-click > Restore
- Folder rollback: Admin > Tools > Folder Rollback (restores folder to point-in-time)
- Full account: Contact support for full account restoration from geo-redundant backup

## Disaster Recovery SLA
- RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 1 hour
- RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 4 hours
- 99.99% durability guarantee

# Mobile App Guide (iOS & Android)

## Installation
- iOS: App Store > search 'CloudSync Pro'
- Android: Google Play > search 'CloudSync Pro'
- MDM deployment: Available via Apple Business Manager and Android Enterprise

## Features
- Browse and search all cloud files
- Offline access: Star files/folders for offline availability
- Photo auto-upload: Settings > Photos > Auto Upload
- Document scanning: Tap + > Scan Document
- Share to CloudSync: Use system share sheet from any app

## Settings
- Upload on cellular: Settings > Network > Cellular Upload (default: off)
- Photo upload quality: Settings > Photos > Quality (Original/High/Medium)
- Passcode lock: Settings > Security > App Lock
- Cache size: Settings > Storage > Cache Limit

## Troubleshooting
- App crashes: Clear cache (Settings > Storage > Clear Cache) or reinstall
- Sync issues: Pull down to refresh, check network connection
- Upload stuck: Check network settings, ensure sufficient cloud storage
